flowSpec.goal: Execute a full Software Development Lifecycle to build a marketing website for CloudPulse, a cloud monitoring SaaS platform. Follow all phases in order: requirements, design, task planning, implementation, QA, and iterative review. Produce a single index.html file that meets all quality gates.

source.text: "You are a brand strategist. Generate comprehensive brand guidelines for CloudPulse, a cloud monitoring SaaS platform. Include: company mission (real-time cloud infrastructure visibility), primary color

position.text: let brand = prompt "You are a brand strategist. Generate comprehensive brand guidelines for CloudPulse, a cloud monitoring SaaS platform. Include: company mission (real-time cloud infrastructure visibility), primary color #2563EB (electric blue), secondary color #1E40AF (deep blue), accent color #10B981 (emerald green for success states), neutral palette (slate grays), typography recommendations (modern sans-serif like Inter or system fonts), tone of voice (professional but approachable, technically credible), logo usage rules, spacing/layout principles. Format as a structured markdown document."

source.text: "You are a product manager. Generate a detailed requirements document for the CloudPulse marketing website. The site must be a single index.html file with inline CSS (no external dependencies). Required sections: (1) Navigation bar with logo and anchor links, (2) Hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA button, (3) Features section with at least 6 feature cards covering real-time monitoring, alerting, dashboards, multi-cloud support, team collaboration, and API access, (4) Pricing section with 3 tiers (Starter/Pro/Enterprise), (5) Testimonials section with at least 3 customer quotes, (6) Integrations section showing supported cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP), (7) FAQ section with at least 5 questions, (8) Footer with CTA, links, and copyright. Acceptance criteria: responsive design (mobile-first), semantic HTML5, accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA), brand color

position.text: let requirements = prompt "You are a product manager. Generate a detailed requirements document for the CloudPulse marketing website. The site must be a single index.html file with inline CSS (no external dependencies). Required sections: (1) Navigation bar with logo and anchor links, (2) Hero section with headline, subheadline, and CTA button, (3) Features section with at least 6 feature cards covering real-time monitoring, alerting, dashboards, multi-cloud support, team collaboration, and API access, (4) Pricing section with 3 tiers (Starter/Pro/Enterprise), (5) Testimonials section with at least 3 customer quotes, (6) Integrations section showing supported cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP), (7) FAQ section with at least 5 questions, (8) Footer with CTA, links, and copyright. Acceptance criteria: responsive design (mobile-first), semantic HTML5, accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA), brand color #2563EB used throughout, minimum 5000 characters total, professional production quality. Format as structured markdown with numbered acceptance criteria."

source.text: You are a UI designer. Using the brand guidelines in brand.md and requirements in requirements.md, create a detailed component design document for the CloudPulse marketing website. For each section (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ, footer), specify: HTML structure (semantic elements to use), CSS approach (flexbox/grid layouts, responsive breakpoints at 768px and 1024px), color application (which brand colors where), spacing (consistent padding/margin system using 8px grid), typography (font sizes, weights, line heights), interactive states (hover effects on buttons and cards), and visual hierarchy. Also specify the overall page structure: max-width container (1200px), section spacing, and the responsive mobile menu approach. Format as structured markdown.

position.text: let design = prompt "You are a UI designer. Using the brand guidelines in brand.md and requirements in requirements.md, create a detailed component design document for the CloudPulse marketing website. For each section (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ, footer), specify: HTML structure (semantic elements to use), CSS approach (flexbox/grid layouts, responsive breakpoints at 768px and 1024px), color application (which brand colors where), spacing (consistent padding/margin system using 8px grid), typography (font sizes, weights, line heights), interactive states (hover effects on buttons and cards), and visual hierarchy. Also specify the overall page structure: max-width container (1200px), section spacing, and the responsive mobile menu approach. Format as structured markdown."

source.text: You are a technical lead. Based on the requirements in requirements.md and design in design.md, generate a numbered task list for building the CloudPulse marketing website. Each task should be a single implementable unit. Use this exact format — one task per line, numbered: 1. Create base HTML structure with doctype head and meta tags 2. Build navigation bar with logo and anchor links 3. Build hero section with headline subheadline and CTA 4. Build features section with 6 feature cards in responsive grid 5. Build pricing section with 3 tier cards 6. Build testimonials section with customer quotes 7. Build integrations section with cloud provider logos 8. Build FAQ section with expandable questions 9. Build footer with CTA links and copyright 10. Add responsive CSS for mobile breakpoints 11. Add hover effects and transitions 12. Add accessibility attributes and skip navigation. Output ONLY the numbered list, no other text.

position.text: let tasks = prompt "You are a technical lead. Based on the requirements in requirements.md and design in design.md, generate a numbered task list for building the CloudPulse marketing website. Each task should be a single implementable unit. Use this exact format — one task per line, numbered: 1. Create base HTML structure with doctype head and meta tags 2. Build navigation bar with logo and anchor links 3. Build hero section with headline subheadline and CTA 4. Build features section with 6 feature cards in responsive grid 5. Build pricing section with 3 tier cards 6. Build testimonials section with customer quotes 7. Build integrations section with cloud provider logos 8. Build FAQ section with expandable questions 9. Build footer with CTA links and copyright 10. Add responsive CSS for mobile breakpoints 11. Add hover effects and transitions 12. Add accessibility attributes and skip navigation. Output ONLY the numbered list, no other text."

body.text: You are a senior frontend developer. Implement this task for the CloudPulse marketing website: ${task}. Read the current index.html if it exists and ADD to it (do not overwrite previous work). Follow the brand guidelines in brand.md (primary color

position.text: prompt: You are a senior frontend developer. Implement this task for the CloudPulse marketing website: ${task}. Read the current index.html if it exists and ADD to it (do not overwrite previous work). Follow the brand guidelines in brand.md (primary color #2563EB, secondary #1E40AF, accent #10B981) and the component design in design.md. Write all code to index.html as a single self-contained file with inline CSS in a style tag. Use semantic HTML5, ensure responsive design, and include ARIA attributes for accessibility. If index.html does not exist yet, create it with the full HTML5 boilerplate including viewport meta tag, charset, and title "CloudPulse - Cloud Monitoring Made Simple".

nodes.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); if(!fs.existsSync('index.html')){process.exit(1)} const h=fs.readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); if(h.length<500){process.exit(1)} console.log('index.html exists: '+h.length+' chars')"

position.text: run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); if(!fs.existsSync('index.html')){process.exit(1)} const h=fs.readFileSync('index.html','utf8'); if(h.length<500){process.exit(1)} console.log('index.html exists: '+h.length+' chars')"

thenBranch.text: index.html is missing or too small. Create a complete CloudPulse marketing website in index.html with all required sections (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ, footer). Use brand color

position.text: prompt: index.html is missing or too small. Create a complete CloudPulse marketing website in index.html with all required sections (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, integrations, FAQ, footer). Use brand color #2563EB. Make it a single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS. It must be at least 5000 characters and include responsive design.

run: node -e "const r=['Structure: ${qa_structure}','Accessibility: ${qa_a11y}','SEO: ${qa_seo}','Brand: ${qa_brand}'].join('
'); require('fs').writeFileSync('qa-report.md','# QA Report

'+r+'
'); console.log(r)"

thenBranch.text: The structure check found issues: ${qa_structure}. Read index.html and fix these structural problems. Ensure all required sections are present with proper HTML elements. Do not remove existing content.

position.text: prompt: The structure check found issues: ${qa_structure}. Read index.html and fix these structural problems. Ensure all required sections are present with proper HTML elements. Do not remove existing content.

thenBranch.text: The accessibility check found issues: ${qa_a11y}. Read index.html and fix these accessibility problems. Add missing ARIA attributes, alt text, semantic elements, and viewport meta tag as needed. Do not remove existing content.

position.text: prompt: The accessibility check found issues: ${qa_a11y}. Read index.html and fix these accessibility problems. Add missing ARIA attributes, alt text, semantic elements, and viewport meta tag as needed. Do not remove existing content.

thenBranch.text: The SEO check found issues: ${qa_seo}. Read index.html and fix these SEO problems. Add missing title, meta description, charset, heading hierarchy as needed. Do not remove existing content.

position.text: prompt: The SEO check found issues: ${qa_seo}. Read index.html and fix these SEO problems. Add missing title, meta description, charset, heading hierarchy as needed. Do not remove existing content.

thenBranch.text: The brand compliance check found issues: ${qa_brand}. Read index.html and fix these brand issues. Ensure "CloudPulse" appears in the content, the primary brand color

position.text: prompt: The brand compliance check found issues: ${qa_brand}. Read index.html and fix these brand issues. Ensure "CloudPulse" appears in the content, the primary brand color #2563EB is used, CTA buttons are present, and the file is at least 5000 characters. Do not remove existing content.

source.text: You are a senior QA reviewer performing Verification and Validation. Read index.html and compare it against the requirements in requirements.md. Check every acceptance criterion. For each criterion, mark it PASS or FAIL with a brief explanation. At the end, if ALL criteria pass, write exactly APPROVED on its own line. If any criteria fail, write REJECTED followed by a numbered list of specific fixes needed. Be thorough but fair — minor cosmetic issues should not cause rejection.

position.text: let review = prompt "You are a senior QA reviewer performing Verification and Validation. Read index.html and compare it against the requirements in requirements.md. Check every acceptance criterion. For each criterion, mark it PASS or FAIL with a brief explanation. At the end, if ALL criteria pass, write exactly APPROVED on its own line. If any criteria fail, write REJECTED followed by a numbered list of specific fixes needed. Be thorough but fair — minor cosmetic issues should not cause rejection."

source.text: Based on the review feedback below, generate a concise numbered list of specific fix tasks. Each task should be one actionable item. Review feedback: ${review}. Output ONLY the numbered list.

position.text: let fix_tasks = prompt "Based on the review feedback below, generate a concise numbered list of specific fix tasks. Each task should be one actionable item. Review feedback: ${review}. Output ONLY the numbered list."

body.text: You are a senior frontend developer. Fix this issue in index.html: ${fix}. Read the current file, make the targeted fix, and write it back. Follow brand guidelines (primary

position.text: prompt: You are a senior frontend developer. Fix this issue in index.html: ${fix}. Read the current file, make the targeted fix, and write it back. Follow brand guidelines (primary #2563EB). Do not remove or break existing working sections. Make minimal changes to address the specific issue.

nodes.text: Summarize the SDLC process that was executed. List: (1) the key design decisions captured, (2) how many tasks were planned and implemented, (3) the QA results from qa-report.md, (4) whether the review loop approved the result or how many iterations were needed, and (5) the final file size and section count of index.html.

position.text: prompt: Summarize the SDLC process that was executed. List: (1) the key design decisions captured, (2) how many tasks were planned and implemented, (3) the QA results from qa-report.md, (4) whether the review loop approved the result or how many iterations were needed, and (5) the final file size and section count of index.html.
